title: Add lightweight in-run monitoring snapshots
problem: Full audit-run sampling became increasingly slow during the 16-paper run, and in-progress audits report status unknown until summary.json exists. Manual samples can become stale while a long Codex subprocess finishes.
proposed_change: Add a cheap monitor command that reads events.jsonl, current graph size, latest validation report, and active paper state without running full graph audits; write periodic JSONL samples with a clear running/completed status.
acceptance_criteria: During a run, monitor samples complete quickly, show running status before summary.json exists, identify the active paper and last event, and do not require recomputing expensive review queues unless requested.

observations.context: Manual monitoring wrote 21 JSONL samples during ag95-diverse-003-16p. Full audit sampling became several seconds slower as the graph grew, in-progress status was unknown before summary.json existed, and sample-020 became stale while mr4792069 finished between sampling and note writing. A cheap event-tail monitor would avoid these issues.

Implemented monitor-run and used it during ag95-diverse-004-replay-16p. Samples showed running status without summary.json, progress 9/16 through 15/16, active paper detection, graph counts, and latest per-paper warning deltas from skipped-delta event payloads; final sample reported completed with 16 papers, 434 nodes, 740 edges.

history.note: Implemented quiver tool monitor-run with optional JSONL append and validated it during ag95-diverse-004-replay-16p.
